response: The internet originated from 1960s U.S. Department of Defense computer networking research, with its precursor ARPANET first demonstrated in 1969, before the 1983 transition to TCP/IP protocols established the foundation for the modern internet. Subsequent landmark developments included Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web, the 1993 launch of the accessible Mosaic browser, the late 1990s dot-com boom and 2001 crash that produced enduring corporate giants, plus the rise of social media, smartphones and cloud computing through the 2000s. Today, the internet connects over 5 billion people worldwide, and underpins almost every area of modern life including commerce, communication and entertainment.
